greeting.title: "Safety Protected by AI, a Future Opened by Technology"
greeting.intro: Building on a wealth of R&D achievements and product commercialization experience, Thingswell is growing into an on-device AI spatial computing company that leads innovation in industrial and everyday safety. What we pursue is not technology for its own sake, but safety solutions that protect human life and earn trust even in extreme environments. hero.desc: A next-generation industrial safety solution that integrates AI cameras, smart bands, environmental sensors, and fire detection systems. Edge AI-based real-time hazard detection and automatic alarms protect workers' lives and safety. It operates independently on-site even during network outages, ensuring safety in any situation.

products.title: AI Safety Camera
products.desc: Detects worker falls, collapses, and prolonged immobility in real time using deep learning-based video analytics. NVIDIA DeepStream-based multi-channel concurrent analysis processes 8 cameras on a single server.

products.title: Smart Safety Band
products.desc: Monitors workers' vital signs (heart rate, body temperature) and location in real time. Provides a fall-detection accelerometer, SOS emergency calls, and danger-zone proximity alerts.

products.title: Environmental Safety Sensor
products.desc: Measures temperature, humidity, gas concentration, and dust levels in real time to assess workplace risk. LoRa/WiFi wireless communication allows installation without wiring.

products.title: Fire Detection System
products.desc: Applies multiple detection methods-heat, smoke, and flame-to detect fires early. Dry-contact integration instantly links with existing firefighting equipment and triggers automatic alarms.

cards.subtitle: A Rapid Bodyguard for On-Site Accident Prevention
description: In an era where advanced technology takes responsibility for safety, 'On-Device AI' has emerged to transform the paradigm of industrial sites. Like a reliable bodyguard for on-site workers, this technology instantly detects hazards and prevents accidents before they occur.

The greatest appeal of On-Device AI is its 'speed.' Conventional cloud-based AI inevitably suffers delays because it must send data to a server for analysis, but On-Device AI processes everything on the device itself. At sites where instantaneous accidents such as falls or collisions occur, decisions made within milliseconds (ms) make a critical difference in saving lives.

In addition, this technology adapts perfectly to diverse environments. By fusing visual information from RGB cameras with ToF (distance measurement) and radar (motion detection) sensor data, it detects hazards without gaps even in dark environments or in underground spaces and tunnels where communication is cut off. The moment a worker collapses or enters a restricted area, it immediately delivers audible or visual warnings to alert of the danger.

On-Device AI goes beyond mere technology, presenting a new standard for industrial sites that put safety first. Now, the power of technology makes it possible to build safer and more efficient work environments.

s1.heading: Overview
s1.text: We develop On-Device Artificial Intelligence technology specialized for the industrial safety field, providing solutions that can detect hazards in real time and respond immediately even in unstable or limited communication environments. By processing sensor data and AI algorithms directly on site, we simultaneously achieve delay-free decision-making, privacy protection, and low-power operation.

s2.heading: Technology Composition and Features
rows: High-performance edge hardware based on NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, Rockchip, and ARM
Real-time AI model processing of sensor data such as camera/radar/ToF
RGB cameras, ToF (Time of Flight), radar, IMU, gas sensors, and more
Accurate and highly reliable hazard assessment through composite sensor fusion
Voice guidance, high-brightness LED warnings, wireless transmitter/relay control
Immediate delivery of warning and control signals on site without an external communication network
Various module form factors such as mobile cams, mount cams, and smart sensors
Flexible installation and functional expansion tailored to site conditions

items.desc: — Detects various behaviors such as collapses, restricted-area entry, and rescue signals (waving hands) in real time. Operates efficiently in an on-device environment with lightweight models (YOLOv11, MediaPipe, Pose Estimation, etc.).
— Recognizes movement in non-visible areas using radar and ToF to detect abnormal situations, and immediately alerts with audible/visual warnings.

items.term: Smart PPE Integration and Data Logging
items.desc: — Links mobile sensors (temperature/humidity, gas, location, etc.) via BLE/MQTT, and automatically reports/alerts and accumulates logs when anomalies are detected.
— Operates fully independently even at sites with weak communication infrastructure, ensuring stable operation.

s4.heading: Technological Differentiation
rows: Minimizes external transmission of video/audio data
Possibility of personal data leakage during transmission
Immediate response within a few ms
Delays of several seconds or more depending on network conditions
Requires building power and communication infrastructure

s5.heading: Applicable Industrial Environments
items: Construction sites: Detection of risky behavior in elevated work, monitoring of approach to non-visible areas
Manufacturing plants: Detection of approach to hazardous zones of equipment/heavy machinery and monitoring of abnormal behavior
Tunnels/underground worksites: Independent hazard detection and warning in communication-blocked zones
Logistics centers/warehouses: Detection of forklift-worker collision risks, monitoring of restricted-access areas
Local governments/public facilities: Detection of emergencies such as fire and flooding

s6.heading: Service Offerings
items: Development of On-Device AI-based POC products and systems
Design and optimization of site-tailored sensor combinations
AI model optimization services based on Jetson Orin and Rockchip
Industrial safety AI performance testing and KOLAS compliance support
Edge-to-Cloud integration and administrator dashboard development

cards.subtitle: Creating a Healthy and Comfortable Living Environment
description: 'AI-Based Air Quality Management Technology' is an innovative system that leverages machine learning to enhance indoor and outdoor air quality data and proactively control a comfortable environment. This technology autonomously resolves sensor errors and aging issues to secure data 'reliability,' providing a foundation that can be utilized for public projects and precise environmental analysis.

In particular, machine learning-based 'anomaly detection' technology automatically corrects errors caused by sensor contamination or aging, always maintaining accurate information. In addition, 'predictive maintenance technology' predicts the possibility of device failure in advance, reducing unnecessary site visits and management costs. 'Smart environmental control technology' provides proactive services by linking with air purifiers, air conditioners, and more—automatically activating when fine dust surges or optimally controlling ventilation systems according to carbon dioxide levels. This technology is applied to a wide range of spaces, from environmentally sensitive facilities such as daycare centers, hospitals, and schools to smart apartments and shopping complexes, contributing to creating user-tailored, safe, and healthy living environments.

s1.heading: Technology Concept and Acquisition Status
s1.p1a: This technology is an innovative unit technology that combines
s1.p1b: and Predictive Maintenance technology to enhance indoor and outdoor air quality data and proactively control a comfortable environment. Through the 2019 Leading-Type Startup Project of the Startup Growth Technology Development Program, we secured core technological competitiveness for developing a highly reliable indoor/outdoor air quality management system and a comfortable environment control service.
s1.p2: This core technology autonomously resolves sensor errors and aging issues to maximize data reliability and predicts the possibility of device failure in advance. It also aims to be applied to solutions that create safe and healthy living spaces through customized environmental control for environmentally sensitive groups.

s2.heading: Features and Strengths of the Core Technology

sub1.heading: Securing Data Reliability: Machine Learning-Based Anomaly Detection Technology
sub1.text: Machine learning models automatically identify and correct data errors that occur as sensors become contaminated or age. This provides always-accurate and reliable air quality data, which can be utilized for public projects and precise environmental analysis.

sub2.heading: Maximizing Maintenance Efficiency: Predictive Maintenance Technology
sub2.text: By analyzing air quality data together with device operating information, it predicts sensor performance degradation and failure timing in advance. This is applied to solutions that reduce unnecessary site visits and management costs by notifying users in advance of issues such as device fan abnormalities or filter replacement timing.

sub3.heading: User-Tailored Environmental Control: Smart Environmental Control Technology
sub3.text: By considering accumulated data and the characteristics of environmentally sensitive groups, it contributes to deriving optimal comfort conditions by group and by building. It is a core component of solutions that provide proactive environmental control services, such as linking with air purifiers and air conditioners to automatically activate when fine dust surges, or optimally controlling ventilation systems according to carbon dioxide levels.

s3.heading: Core Technology Development Details and System Logic Structure
s3.text: This core technology was completed through the following development process and implemented advanced functions through a logical data processing structure.

items.term: Development of Fine Dust Data Correction Technology:
items.desc: Collected and analyzed normal and abnormal fine dust sensor datasets to develop a machine learning model.
Developed a model that predicts abnormal signs of sensors and devices using machine learning-based Anomaly Detection technology.

items.term: Development of a Universal Legacy Adapter:
items.desc: Developed a universal adapter to link various previously installed environmental equipment (air purifiers, air conditioners, etc.) with this system, securing compatibility.

sub2.heading: AI-Based Air Quality Management System Process

items.desc: Collects real-time data such as fine dust, carbon dioxide, and temperature/humidity from indoor and outdoor air quality measurement devices.
The collected data is identified and corrected for abnormal data through a machine learning-based Anomaly Detection algorithm.
Based on the corrected data, derives optimal comfort conditions suited to environmentally sensitive groups and analyzes device status with a predictive maintenance model.

Based on the analysis results, automatically controls environmental equipment linked with air purifiers, air conditioners, and more, and explains the solution's operation process of notifying users when maintenance is needed.

s4.heading: Application Targets and Expected Effects
s4.text: This technology is the core technology of solutions successfully applied to a variety of spaces where air quality management is essential.

sub1.heading: Main Application Targets
sub1.text: Environmentally sensitive facilities such as daycare centers, nursing facilities, hospitals, and schools; smart apartments; factory office buildings; urban buildings with heavy traffic; shopping complexes; and local government air-environment precision management projects, among others

items.desc: Contributes to automatically maintaining the best air quality by considering user characteristics and the spatial environment.
Enables solutions that improve management efficiency by predicting device failures and notifying maintenance timing.
Plays a key role in implementing solutions that enable proactive responses to the inflow of external pollutants by analyzing outdoor air quality as well.

s4.textEnd: Based on these strengths, this technology has secured competitiveness in the smart air quality management and care solution market.

cards.subtitle: An Intelligent Safety Partner for the Field
description: Today, safety at industrial sites requires not just simple monitoring but 'intelligence that understands and judges situations.'

Conventional safety systems only provided warnings according to fixed rules, but now a 'conversational safety awareness system' is needed—one that understands workers' questions and presents solutions for complex situations.

At the heart of this innovation is RAG (Retrieve-Augment-Generate)-based LLM (Large Language Model) technology. By combining vast manuals, guidelines, and real-time sensor data, this technology can even answer the question 'Why.'

For example, in response to a worker's question 'The machine is making a strange noise,' it can respond quickly and accurately, such as 'There is a possibility of motor overheating, so cut off the power immediately.'
In particular, with on-device inference that operates independently even in underground worksites or tunnels where communication is cut off, it eliminates safety blind spots.

RAG-based LLM is not a mere observer but an 'intelligent safety partner' that predicts hazards, communicates, and provides field knowledge in real time, presenting a new paradigm for industrial safety.

s1.heading: Overview
s1.text: We provide a conversational safety awareness system that utilizes RAG (Retrieve-Augment-Generate)-based LLM (Large Language Model) technology so that complex hazardous situations at industrial sites can be understood and responded to in real time. By combining situational awareness, context analysis, and knowledge retrieval, this solution is a next-generation industrial safety solution that enables intelligent warnings, reporting, and response guidance.

s2.heading: Core Technology Composition
rows: In-house optimized models of the GPT, phi, and Llama families
Worker Q&A and natural-language explanations of hazardous situations
Response generation based on an on-device vector database and document retrieval technology
Automated explanation based on field guidelines and provision of accurate information
Operates on low-power edge devices such as NVIDIA Jetson Orin
Performs LLM inference independently even in environments where communication is difficult

items.term: Real-Time Interpretation and Guidance of Hazardous Situations
items.desc: — e.g., When 'The machine is making a strange noise' is entered, provides guidance through context analysis such as 'There is a possibility of motor overheating. Cut off the power immediately and call the manager.'
— Generates situation-tailored voice warnings in real time, such as 'You are approaching a hazardous zone. Move to the left,' by linking with radar/ToF detection events.
— In emergencies such as 'A person has collapsed,' searches the guideline manual to provide step-by-step first-aid guidance.
— Based on a report such as 'Hazardous material leak occurred,' automatically generates a hazard report according to a predefined template to simplify procedures.

s4.heading: Technological Differentiation
rows: Understands context and provides knowledge-based explanations
Centered on fixed responses, lacking flexibility
Utilizes built-in guidelines/work manuals via RAG
Dependent on knowledge external to the system
Local inference possible → overcomes communication constraints

s5.heading: Application Fields
items: Construction sites: Risk behavior Q&A, safety education support for subcontracted workers
Manufacturing plants: Explanation of equipment anomaly signs, response to work manual queries
Nighttime unmanned worksites: Situation interpretation and response guidance after hazard detection
Public facilities/underground spaces: Voice-conversation-based response guidance in emergencies

s6.heading: Available Services
items: Customization of an integrated RAG + LLM conversational system
Construction of a vector database based on field safety manuals
Provision of models optimized for on-device platforms such as Jetson Orin
Development of voice input/output UI and multilingual support systems
Production of real-world demonstration scenarios and POC (Proof of Concept) testing support

cards.subtitle: A Reliable Safety Guardian for Industrial Sites
description: 'Multimodal Situational Awareness Technology' is an innovative system that elevates industrial site safety to a new level. Like the human eyes and ears, this technology comprehensively perceives and fuses diverse information from RGB cameras, ToF (distance measurement) sensors, UWB (ultra-wideband) radar sensors, and more.

Even in environments that were difficult to handle with cameras alone—such as changes in illumination, dust, and occlusion—it can detect accurately without being affected by light, boasting powerful adaptability.

The core of this technology lies in 'On-Device AI.' On site, it immediately detects abnormal behaviors such as a worker collapsing or falling in real time, and precisely identifies the hazardous approach of heavy machinery to prevent collision accidents. In particular, by utilizing radar to detect even invisible blind spots, it plays a major role in minimizing safety blind spots.

When a hazardous situation occurs, it immediately delivers warning alerts to workers via voice, display, mobile app, and more, and by linking with smart PPE (personal protective equipment), it monitors even hazardous gases and temperature changes to protect workers more safely. Multimodal fusion technology that goes beyond the limits of a single sensor is expected to raise industrial site safety to a new level.

s1.heading: Overview
s1.text: To secure industrial site safety in real time, we are developing and commercializing multimodal situational awareness technology that fuses RGB cameras, Time-of-Flight (ToF) sensors, and UWB Radar sensors. This technology overcomes the limitations of conventional single-sensor-based detection, enabling accurate hazard recognition and response even in diverse environments such as changes in illumination, occlusion, and dust.

s2.heading: Core Technology Composition
rows: Distance-based approach detection, hazardous distance detection
Detection of non-visible areas in occluded environments

items.term: Worker Behavior Detection and Hazard Warning System
items.desc: — Automatically detects collapses/falls/excessive movement/abnormal behavior to prevent accidents, and provides warning alerts through various channels such as voice, display, and mobile app immediately upon detection.

items.term: Vehicle and Heavy Machinery Approach Hazard Detection
items.desc: — Accurately detects direction and approach speed using radar, and immediately issues warnings when a worker approaches a blind spot to prevent collision accidents.

items.term: Enhanced Monitoring of Dark Environments and Occluded Zones
items.desc: — Overcomes camera limitations through ToF/Radar fusion, performing accurate hazard detection even in low-illumination environments such as enclosed spaces, nighttime, and tunnels.
— Links with BLE-based smart PPE to monitor individual environmental changes, and immediately sends alerts when anomalies such as hazardous gases or temperature changes are detected.

s4.heading: Technological Differentiation
rows: Low (independent of light through combined use of Radar/ToF)
Optimized per situation through fusion of three sensors
Limited by the performance of a single sensor
Based on On-Device AI (Jetson Orin, etc.)
Independent operation based on edge computing

cards.subtitle: The Future of Dignified and Safe Care
description:
'Smart Assistive Technology for the Elderly with Cognitive Impairment and People with Disabilities' is an innovative solution that overcomes the limitations of modern care systems. This technology improves safety by detecting hazardous situations such as falls and wandering—which people with cognitive impairment may experience—at an early stage. In particular, even in privacy-sensitive spaces such as bedrooms and bathrooms where CCTV is difficult to install, it is designed to enable thorough monitoring while protecting individual dignity by utilizing contactless multimodal sensing technology.

This system does not stop at merely detecting hazards. By precisely learning the user's behavior patterns, it predicts abnormal behavior that differs from the usual and immediately delivers alerts to care staff. This greatly reduces the workload of care staff, who find it difficult to be on standby around the clock, and enables rapid responses to unexpected situations. Ultimately, this technology will provide a safe and dignified life for people with cognitive impairment and an efficient and systematic care environment for guardians and administrators, contributing to raising the level of welfare in our society by one step.

s1.heading: Technology Overview
s1.text: We have developed situational/behavioral awareness home care assistive devices and service technology for the daily-life convenience and safety of the elderly and people with disabilities who have cognitive impairment. Through AI-based multimodal sensing, this technology enables thorough safety management while protecting individual privacy. In particular, it is designed to effectively detect and respond to hazardous situations in blind spots such as bedrooms and bathrooms where CCTV is difficult to install.

s2.heading: Necessity and Importance of the Technology
s2.text: Recently, the number of patients with mild cognitive impairment has been rapidly increasing, yet the existing care methods for them have several limitations. Representative examples include a shortage of three-shift staff, difficulty in responding immediately to unexpected situations, and privacy infringement caused by CCTV. Our technology resolves these problems, contributing to enabling users with cognitive impairment to live safely and relieving the burden on guardians and administrators.

s3.heading: Main Technology Composition
s3.p1a: Our AI multimodal home care solution is built around an
s3.p1strong: AI Multimodal Home Care Station (Edge Server)

sub1.heading: Home Care Station PoC

items.desc: A concept prototype that improves space utilization with a wall-mounted or stand type.
A concept prototype of the stand type that can be flexibly placed in various spaces.

sub1.text: This station serves as an edge server that collects and analyzes the behavioral and situational data of users with cognitive impairment, and is driven by the following technologies.

items.desc: Integrates and analyzes multimodal data to accurately recognize the user's behavior and situation, and detects hazardous situations such as falls and wandering in real time.
Learns the user's daily patterns to detect abnormal behavior that differs from the usual and predicts problems caused by disease characteristics.
Provides 24-hour safety management even in CCTV blind spots, preventing falls and other hazardous situations at an early stage.
Enables effective monitoring without infringing on individual privacy by utilizing contactless technology.
Reduces the workload of management staff and improves efficiency through immediate alerts and automatic recording functions for unexpected situations.
Analyzes the user's behavior patterns and health status to provide baseline data for establishing a tailored care plan.

cards.subtitle: Strengthening Patient Safety in Medical Settings
description: Optimized for hospitals and nursing facilities, this solution precisely analyzes patient conditions in real time through a 'composite sensor module that fuses radar and cameras.' Going beyond simple motion detection, an 'AI-based inference engine' autonomously detects and classifies falls, abnormal behavior, and even changes in vital signs, immediately delivering alerts to medical staff.

The most important value is that safety management is possible while 'protecting patient privacy.' Contactless radar technology detects hazards without exposing the patient's body, so it can be used with confidence even in spaces where CCTV installation is inappropriate.

In addition, the 'SW-SoC-based embedded system,' designed to be ultra-compact and low-power, performs immediate analysis and warning on site without relying on a communication network, enabling rapid responses to emergencies. This technology reduces the workload of medical staff while presenting a new standard for patient safety management and contributing to improving the quality of the medical environment.

s1.heading: Introduction
s1.text: One of our core technologies is an embedded system for early detection of hazardous situations using radar and cameras. To ensure patient safety, this system provides a customized solution optimized for the hospital room environment and supports an efficient working environment for medical staff.

sub1.heading: AI-Based Early Hazard Detection Inference Engine

items.desc: Links data collected from radar and cameras to precisely analyze patient conditions.
We have developed and possess an artificial intelligence algorithm that autonomously detects and classifies patient falls, abnormal behavior, changes in vital signs, and more.

sub2.heading: Radar and Camera Composite Sensor Module

items.desc: We possess composite sensor module technology consisting of a 3D millimeter-wave radar chipset with multi-array antennas and a high-sensitivity, high-resolution camera.
We have independently designed an embedded board that integrates a low-power, ultra-compact FPGA-equipped multiprocessor and signal processing functions.
We possess technology that efficiently implements AI models on SW-SoC-based FPGAs and utilizes AI quantization and compilers.
Provides effective technology that detects and prevents serious safety accidents such as falls at an early stage.
Through contactless radar technology, it protects patient privacy while detecting hazardous situations without blind spots.
Reduces the workload of medical staff and enables rapid responses to emergencies through automatic monitoring and alarm functions.

government.overviewText: This program provides subsidies to help small and medium-sized workplaces with limited financial and technical resources adopt smart safety equipment, in order to prevent the various industrial accidents that arise from industrial change and technological advancement. By leveraging digital technologies such as AI, IoT, and video analytics, it detects on-site hazards in real time and prevents safety accidents in advance.
government.target1: Business owners of workplaces with fewer than 50 full-time employees (for the construction industry, work sites are excluded, but the head office may apply)
government.target2: Business owners of companies at or below the small enterprise size criteria under the Framework Act on Small and Medium Enterprises (SME confirmation certificate required)
government.target3: Workplaces that own equipment specified in Appendix 21 of the Enforcement Decree of the Occupational Safety and Health Act, or that operate a related leasing business
government.condition1: Subsidies are paid after the selection, contract, installation, and inspection procedures are completed.
government.condition2: Duplicate subsidies from other programs for the same equipment are restricted.
government.condition3: Detailed support ratios and limits follow the criteria announced by the agency.
government.itemsTitle: Supported Items · Support Limit · Support Scope
government.safetyItems: Forklift and heavy-equipment human detection systems, moving-object proximity alarms, collision prevention solutions, hazard zone access control, fall and entrapment prevention devices, video-based worker protection (PPE compliance checks), safety railings/safety gates, conveyor safety devices, temporary facilities and pedestrian protection equipment, and more
government.healthItems: Smart noise, dust, and hazardous gas meters, reinforcement of ventilation and dust collection systems, assistive devices for musculoskeletal-burden tasks, improvement of local exhaust ventilation systems, and more
government.etcItems: Detailed items, support limits, and self-payment ratios follow the agency's annual announcement criteria, and may be adjusted based on prior diagnosis and on-site conditions.
